title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot interrogates Samuel Kid and Jenny Hobbs regarding their conspiracy to murder Harriet Sipper, Adah Gransbury, and Richard Nigges. During the questioning, Kid claims he only entered room 238, where he found two keys and a third key hidden behind a fireplace tile near Nigges's body. 

Poirot exposes a logical flaw in the conspirators' timeline. Jenny had claimed she would meet Kid at the "Joyful Coffee House" at 7:30 p.m. to hear a prepared story, but Poirot notes that the travel time from the Bluxton Hotel makes it impossible for her to commit the murders and arrive at the cafe by that time. Kid attempts to defend the plan by stating the executions were originally scheduled for 6:00 p.m. to ensure Jenny could reach the cafe by 7:30 p.m. However, Poirot questions why Kid was instructed to place a note at the hotel front desk at 8:00 p.m. if the murders had already been completed.

Poirot deduces that the conspirators ordered afternoon tea to Room 317 to create a false alibi, making it appear the victims were still alive at 7:15 p.m. He argues that the victims did not actually die between 7:15 p.m. and 8:10 p.m., but that the conspirators went to great lengths to frame the time of death during a window when Jenny had an unbreakable alibi: being with Poirot at the "Joyful Coffee House" from 7:35 p.m. to 7:50 p.m. Poirot concludes that the effort to prove Jenny's innocence during that specific timeframe suggests she is the killer. Jane reacts by vehemently denying the murders and insisting the deaths occurred between 7:15 p.m. and 8:00 p.m.
